evals.name: go-boundary-violations
evals.prompt:
I've staged some changes to an order service. Take a look before I push.

The repo is at /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/go-order-service
evals.expected_output: Report should identify: dependency direction violation (domain importing database/sql), unnecessary export (FormatPrice only used in-package), upstream knowledge comment ('Called by the OrderHandler'), Law of Demeter (method chain GetCustomer().GetAddress().GetZipCode()), circular dependency (domain imports service), and debug fmt.Println. Should use correct severity format.
expectations: The review identifies that domain/order.go imports database/sql, which is a dependency direction violation — the domain layer should not depend on infrastructure
The review identifies or questions the FormatPrice export — either flagging it as unnecessarily exported or asking whether it's used outside the service package
The review identifies the comment 'Called by the OrderHandler in api/handler.go' as encoding upstream caller knowledge that will become stale
The review identifies the fmt.Println debug line and either removes it or flags it for removal

The review identifies a Law of Demeter concern with the method chains order.GetCustomer().GetAddress().GetZipCode() in service/pricing.go — the caller reaches through multiple objects instead of asking its direct collaborator
The review identifies the circular dependency in domain/validation.go which imports the service package, creating a domain ↔ service import cycle
The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)

evals.name: ts-test-boundary-violations
evals.prompt:
Hey, got some changes staged on the user module. Can you review before I open a PR?

Repo: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/ts-user-module
evals.expected_output: Report should identify: test importing from internal paths, upstream knowledge comment ('Used by the UserRegistration component'), leaky barrel export (export *), and the DI anti-pattern (constructing PostgresClient internally via require('pg')).
expectations: The review identifies the test file importing from '../src/internal/helpers' as a test boundary violation — tests should import through the public barrel (../src)
The review identifies the comment 'Used by the UserRegistration component in pages/register.tsx and the AdminPanel' as upstream knowledge that will rot
The review identifies the 'export * from ./internal/helpers' in index.ts as leaking internal implementation details through the barrel
The review identifies the constructor doing require('pg') and new Client() as a DI anti-pattern — the audit database client should be injected, not constructed internally
The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)

evals.name: go-clean-code-no-false-positives
evals.prompt:
Review my staged changes please.

Repo: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/go-clean-service
evals.expected_output: Report should recognize well-structured code. Should NOT flag false positive boundary violations. May note minor issues like placeholder generateID() or the TODO-like comment about uuid. Should not invent problems.
expectations: The review does NOT flag a dependency direction violation — the adapter correctly imports from domain and usecase, not the other way around
The review does NOT flag the unexported helper functions (normalizeEmail, isValidEmail, generateID) as issues — they are correctly unexported
The review recognizes the interface-based DI pattern as correct (UserRepository and Notifier interfaces in usecase, implemented by adapter)
The review notes the placeholder generateID() implementation as incomplete work or flags the 'In production, use uuid.New().String()' comment
The report still performs standard checks (error handling, edge cases) and doesn't only focus on boundary issues

evals.name: ts-clean-code-no-false-positives
evals.prompt:
Can you review my staged changes? Want to make sure everything looks good before merging.

Repo: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/ts-clean-module
evals.expected_output: Report should recognize well-structured TypeScript code with proper DI, barrel exports, and tests through the public interface. Should NOT flag false positive boundary violations. Code is genuinely clean — should not invent problems.
expectations: The review does NOT flag a dependency direction violation — the service imports only types and interfaces, not infrastructure
The review does NOT flag the barrel export as leaking internals — the barrel explicitly re-exports only public types (no wildcard, no internal paths)
The review recognizes the interface-based DI pattern as correct (NotificationSender and NotificationStore interfaces injected via constructor)
The review does NOT flag test imports as boundary violations — tests import through the public barrel (../src), not internal paths
The report does NOT invent issues — it either reports no behavior-change issues or only reports genuine concerns (not false boundary violations)

evals.name: go-revert-detection
evals.prompt:
Review my staged changes before I push.

Repo: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/go-revert-service
evals.expected_output: The Revert Detection agent should flag that the removed nil-check error handling was added in a prior fix commit. Should not flag unrelated changes as reversions.
expectations: The review identifies that removed error handling (or similar deleted defensive code) was added in a commit with a fix/workaround/regression signal in its message
The review cites the relevant git commit context as evidence for why the removal is suspicious
The review does NOT flag every deletion as a potential reversion — it uses judgment to distinguish deliberate removals from suspicious ones
The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)

evals.name: go-behavioral-compat
evals.prompt:
Can you look over my staged changes?

Repo: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/prepare-staged-for-review-workspace/test-repos/go-behavioral-compat-service
evals.expected_output: The Caller Audit agent should flag that callers depend on nil-vs-empty-slice semantics or specific error types that the staged changes alter, even though the function signature is unchanged.
expectations: The review identifies a behavioral compatibility issue where a caller depends on return value semantics (e.g., nil vs empty slice, sentinel error value) that changed without a signature change
The review cites specific callers that exhibit the behavioral dependency (with file:line references)
The review does NOT flag the change as a signature/API breaking change — it correctly identifies this as a behavioral contract issue
The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
